reasoning: OMC recently jumped from ~$71 to ~$74.15 on news that its $13.25B stock-for-stock acquisition of IPG (announced Dec 2024) cleared major regulatory hurdles and is launching exchange offers. The market close price on 2025-08-17 will be anchored to August 15's close of 74.15. The linear/statistical analysis gives a roughly 57% chance of upward continuation in the very short term, supported by recent positive momentum. However, acquirer stocks often face downward pressure around the close of all-stock deals due to arbitrage and dilution. Furthermore, OMC typically pays quarterly dividends in October and January, which will incrementally reduce the share price for the later resolution dates (November and February), offsetting the expected equity risk premium. Given these competing factors—short-term momentum vs. intermediate-term M&A indigestion and dividend drops—I predict probabilities slightly above 50% for the near term, gradually regressing toward 51-52% for the longer dates as uncertainty and dividend impacts weigh on the nominal stock price.
